here with us for a long time. This is why I come back and I even share. Because I know that your marriage that you want, the family that you want, the life that you want will be fueled by the business that you build. And there's no better place to build it than here, with us. Not alone, with us. So as I'm moving through strategic seduction, and I've got this yes at this point, you will see that there is a momentum shift that occurs. And somebody then dives themselves into stage number four of six. Stage number four is compel action. This is when a person leaves the position of being interested, and they move to a position of desire. They have said, I am not interested any longer. I'm not just interested in what you're talking about, Russell. I'm not just interested in what's going on here, click funnels. I'm not just interested in learning about how to sell one to many. I'm not just interested in learning how to be a funnel builder. I'm not just interested in learning how to be a successful entrepreneur or coach here in this community. I'm not interested anymore. What am I? I have desire that has me leaned in. That desire also says the following, that I am 100% clear that I have a problem that I want to solve. We're not having a conversation with a person at this stage who's still figuring out if there's a problem to fix. Like, many of you are already aware of your problem. You already know it needs to be fixed, but you haven't fixed it yet. How many of you have been exposed to the problems that are standing in the way for you already? Being here in this event has already exposed you to the problems that you're going to have to fix in order to get the money that you want and the life that you want? Type in me in the chat if that's you. Like, it's already exposed you. You already know. It's like, we don't have to do any more investing. You're like, I already know. How many of you also know that exposure to those problems? That it's time for you to fix them? Type in hell yes in the chat if you know it's time to fix the problem now that you're aware of the problem. You're not alone. This is good news. That's the point of being here for a couple of days. It's once he gets you to this place of decision. So that you're standing in the place saying, this sucks, that's awesome. Don't want that, do want this. Not gonna be here anymore, go in here. What's about to get there? It's coming. You're about to hear what it is. And it's about pushing you to this new place. The thing in the middle is irrelevant. It's the place that you're going by owning where you are. Now, at this stage in strategic production, we're also saying that you're like 80% sure, 80% sure that the person's product or service that you're looking at is what you want to solve the problem. So we're not completely over the hump. Where we are at is we are now in momentum. This 80% on you or your company as a solution. We still don't have a yes to money yet, but we are moving into a yes pattern. This is when we drive people to sales pages or we have drive people into conversations or one-on-one consulting contract conversations, sales calls or sales pages. Moving into this fifth stage, we're gonna close the commitment. For me, this is me who's sitting in one-on-one calls. They're my favorite. I do a ton of Judah sales pages where we sell one to many and we'll have a ton of sales at once. But my favorite in stage five is to sit down pit-to-peek conversation with a man or a woman and I will map the gap. I will literally take and map the entire problem sequence with them visually, then we'll map the entire solution that they're after and then we'll sit back and look at it. Go, what do you see? I see a big gap. Okay. Well, were you ready to do something about it? Yeah. Well, what are we gonna do? And now we insert the solution. I spend almost no time talking about my programs. I spend all my time building tension between the pit and the peak. Tension between I'm getting out of this place and I'm going here. Just like you've been experiencing with us the last couple of days. A tension point of, I'm not staying here, I'm going here. How many of you have felt that tension? You know exactly what I'm talking about. Type in me. Type in me in the chat if you notice. You feel that tension already happening. You're like, listen, I get it. I'm in the pocket of the tension. I feel it. I'm understanding it. Then in this page in stage five, close commitment is when we get to a place where we're a simple yes or no. No babies. Yes or no? You're either in or you're out. No maybe. Yes or no? The challenge of many of you living is in your mind, you allow yourself to get into the kind of yes. And the people you're surrounded by allow you to live in maybe. You say, are you in? Well, yeah, maybe. Maybe what? Maybe it's not an answer. You're either yes or you're no. I don't care if you're yes or no, but you make a decision. Are you a yes or you're no? You will watch people squirm under this. Human beings don't like to be held accountable to actual committed decisions. Yet the reality is you have to act that way first if you expect other people to do the same. You said yes to being here. You said yes to showing up. You said yes to turning your camera on. You learned how to say yes. But that means you also have to be powerful in saying no. These two equations have to play out yes and no. You have to get to a place in your mind as a man, as a woman, where you do not live in any resemblance at all of maybe. You have to know that when you say you're in, you're in for yourself. That there's integrity. God said, let there be light and there was light. He didn't say let there be light and maybe there'll be some light. Let there be light and there was darkness. Let there be light and there was pink. I don't know how that showed up. There's pink showed up. No. Integrity. What he said happened. This comes down to a line of understanding your life exists in yes or no. Surrounding yourself with people who are maybe people is disgusting. You can't count on anyone because they might do it. I'm in maybe. I was sitting on a sales call with the guy. He said, hey, you know, I like to talk to God about this. These kind of big decisions. I was like, it's a quarter million dollars. I can appreciate that. I said, so why don't we just talk to him right now? He said, well, what do you mean? I was like, I talk to God every day. You talk to God too. That's good news. We'll come from the same cloth. So we were on Zoom. I said, I just closed my, off you go. No, but I looked and I was like, what are you doing? He said, you want me to really pray right now? I was like, yeah, we're in momentum. I already brought you through the cycle. We're going to talk to him right now. So you're a faithful man. You believe in God? What's the problem? Let's go. So he's a little uncomfortable and then he did. And I already knew what his answer was going to be. His God already told me. I was waiting for him to catch up. He's like, should I do this with Garrett and Wicca Boyer? He pauses. I started feeling the warmth. My eyes are closed. I peek at him a little bit. And he's like, yes. And he's like, in Jesus' name, amen. He goes, that was weird. I said, no, that was awesome. I was like, that's called somebody holding you accountable to a yes or no. You're not walking out of here without a yes or no? I told you that when we started the conversation. I said, you're going to make a decision today. Not tomorrow, not next week. You're not going to go talk to your pastor, to go talk to your wife, and I'll go have a conversation with 700 people. You want to talk to God? That's fine. Let's do it right here. Why would you wait? What sort of wait for it? How's it going to get any easier a year from now? Or two years from now? Or two minutes from now? Final stage. And then we're done. Money commits people. You cannot open a window. What if it's a long contract? Take a deposit. I'm taking a deposit on land deals. Everything else, I don't care. Give me a card, 10 grand. Let's go. Why? I need your commitment now. See, when you live in the land of yes or no, it's easy to close the gap. If you're a maybe man or a maybe woman, this isn't possible for you to pull off because you're weak. If you lie all the time and you're in maybe, you're done. If you tell the truth and you live in yes or no, you could hold the same standard for everyone around you. Are you in? Yes or no? Yes. Okay, good. What are you putting it on? And before we get too deep into the training and all that kind of stuff, I want to start with, I wanted Garra to give this presentation, so I wanted to start this presentation talking about you guys. If you've been in my world at all, and you've heard my beliefs, the reason why I do this, I believe that every single one of you guys has been called. You've all been called. And it's interesting because people don't come into my world typically like, I'm trying to figure out how to make money. I'm trying to get rich on the internet. Like that's not the world. There's tons of internet marketing rooms that happens. There's tons of masterminds. You go into those rooms and that's what's happening. Like you see that all the time. The people that are attracted to our world and to our community are different. There are people who heard what Alec Sharfman calls the call to contribution. They have a calling. They feel something. They feel something different. And they're pulled and drawn into this world because they know they've been called to change somebody's life. And I'm the biggest believer of that. Every single one of, I believe every single person has been called. Very few follow calling though. But if you're here today, it means you've felt that calling. You've been called now. The reason why I'm here, why I keep doing this is because I tell people all the time, like my job is to gather the gatherers, help them to empower them so they can go out and they can change the world. Okay. Todd told you it was on day number one, the mission of ClickFunnels is to free all entrepreneurs so you guys can get back to changing lives to the customers you've been called to serve. Right. We do it through tools, through training, through trying to figure out how to break your fault's beliefs. Right. How we break these things through so you can move forward, so you can change people's lives, you can change your life, so you can change your family's life and so on and so forth. I also shared this quote at the very beginning from Alec Charfin, right. We're different, we're unique. And what he said, I think it's so powerful, he said, we are the small percentage of the population that goes into the future, imagines new reality and then comes back to the present and insists it becomes real. That's what makes us different, that's what makes us unique. Okay. Some of you guys may know if you've been following me, any of my social stuff or anything over the last couple of years. A couple of years ago, I read the book Atlas Shrugged. I got obsessed with the concept of Atlas holding the weight of the world on his shoulders. Right. A little while afterwards, one of my friends from high school, I messaged him because he's an actual sculptor. He has all the big statues here in Vegas. If you go to like Sahara and all these different places, he's one of the ones that has all the big sculptors and things like that. Went to high school with him and his brother and I messaged him, I was like, I want to build an Atlas statue someday. I want to build a statue because the people I haven't called to serve are the people who are holding the weight of the world on their shoulders. How many of you guys, by the way, are the person in your life, in your family, your community who are holding the weight of the world on their shoulders every single day? Yeah. I see you guys. I understand that. I feel the exact same way. That's my calling is, all of us, we have the calling, we're holding the weight of the world on our shoulders. So I asked him, can you help build a statue of Atlas holding the weight of the world on their shoulders? He's like, why do you want to do that? I said, I read this book Atlas Shrugged. They got excited about the producers and the prime movers and the people that are changing the world and I want something. He said, well, how about instead of just doing that, what if we made three statues? What are you talking about? He's like, if you've read the book Atlas Shrugged, the premise of the book Atlas Shrugged is what happens if someday of all the producers in the world, they're holding weight of the world on their shoulders, if they get frustrated, right? Because the government regulations have different problems. So they stand up and they shrug and they walk away through the responsibilities. They would do three statues, one of Atlas holding the weight of the world on their shoulders, one with him standing and shrugging, one with him walking away. And obviously I freaked out. I got excited and he spent the next six months or so designing this statue set right here. And this is Atlas holding the weight of the world on her shoulders, standing and shrugging and then walking away. And this is right now, we are building this in the eventual event center for what we are doing to try to change the world we're building right now. When you walk in the front doors, there will be a nine foot statue holding a six foot globe, three of them when you walk in to represent us as the Prime Movers Society. People that are trying to change the world. And my mission is always to help support those of you guys who are here. Because if and when we get to a situation where we stand up and walk away, I fear for the world at that point. And so for me, it's like my calling is to help people who are in this spot who are holding the weight of the world on their shoulders, right? And again, in the book Atlas Shrugged, she calls these people Prime Movers. I thought that was her word. I thought she made that up. And I found out recently at the Atlas event that we had a little while ago, I was talking about this concept of Prime Movers. I was so excited about it. And Eileen Wilder, she messaged me the next night. She's like, guess what? I did a bunch of studying. Turns out, Aristotle was the person who actually first came up with the concept of the Prime Mover. I was like, what? Are you kidding me? She's like, yes. We started researching, geeking out. I go and find like first edition of Aristotle books. Those have been pieces of paper that were destroyed by now. But I found a version in the 1700s of Aristotle's books where he talks about this concept of Prime Mover. It was fascinating the way Aristotle explained. He said, the Prime Mover in the world is God. He says, God is the Prime Mover. And what Prime Movers do not do, God's not down there chasing all of us, trying to get us to do what's right and doing all those kind of things. He says, the Prime Mover stands up there, sets a standard, and gets people to rise to him. I was like, oh my gosh, the symbolism, that's amazing. We talked about what Gareth talked about, strategic seduction. It's not a chasing customer. It's not just trying to get people to do stuff. It's us standing, being setting a standard, and people are coming to us. That's what a Prime Mover is. You probably noticed lately I've been wearing a lot of Prime Mover t-shirts. The audience out here right now is all wearing Prime Mover t-shirts. So I have one here. And I wanted to show this, I'll pull up the slide too so you guys can read it home. But on the front is a picture of the Prime Movers, the Atlas holding the weight of the world on his shoulders. And the back there's a quote, and this quote came from Ayn Rand. And when I read this in the book, it just stuck out to me. That something that was really, really powerful, and like a mission and a mantra for me and for our people. And so this is what it says. It says, throughout the centuries there were men who took first the steps down new roads, armed with nothing but their own vision. Their goals differed, but they all had one thing in common. That the step was first, the road was new, the vision unborrowed. The great creators, the thinkers, the artists, the scientists, the inventors, they stood alone against the men of their time. Every great new thought was opposed, every great new invention was denounced. But the men of unborrowed vision went ahead. They fought, they suffered, and they paid, but they won. And when I read that I was like, that's us. That's our people. That's who is going out there every single day trying to change the world. You are the Prime Movers. And throughout this, as I've been in this community now for over a decade, I've given you guys different identities. I've given you guys identities of a funnel hacker. I've given you guys identities of someone who's driven. And identity I want to give you guys now is that of a Prime Mover. Okay? Someone who's setting a standard different than the rest of the world and getting people to come to you so you can change their lives. Okay? Strategic seduction, how do you get people to start coming towards us? Because those are people we can change our lives. Another concept that Aristotle talked about in this book I was reading, he called it the immovable mover. I thought this was like the most beautiful, like visual ever, right? All of us as the Prime Movers, we are immovable. We are moving the world. We are the immovable movers. I heard that as like, man, that is such a calling to me. How do we do that? How do we change people? How do we get them to move? Right? My earlier session today was about that. It's about how do we take this, right? We have these people, every single person is working with their conscious mind, right? They're thinking they're stuck and they're stuck because they can't move, right? Subconsciously they are stuck. They are not able to move forward, right? If we want to be immovable and we want to move people, we have to learn how to speak, how to communicate, how to persuade, how to get through to people so we can actually break their false beliefs to free them so they can come with us on the journey, right? That's our job. That's our role as the prime movers, okay? And so how do we get them to move? Okay? The process to get them to move is simple. It's three steps. Number one, you have to create an offer. Nothing happens until you make an offer and somebody decides to take you up on that offer and actually move, right? And what we make that offer is we do it by creating a presentation to help break their false belief. I call this the one to many selling presentation. After we've done that, now we can get them on the path to start moving towards the goal that they're looking for, okay? And again, this comes back to everything we talked about right here with subconscious selling from earlier, okay? Now again, just one more way is to bypass the conscious mind, right? So I'm here, I'm telling stories, I'm... Just mind, get to the subconscious so we can find the false belief patterns, replace with the new story. Then they can actually move and you can quiet down fear so they have the... Everything you're putting out there, the goal is that, right? If you don't do that, those beliefs will stop them from buying. Okay? So I decided I would do something a little different. Instead of me just going and like teaching the entire purpose of the webinar, I thought it'd be more fun instead for me to kind of set up the framework really quickly but then actually do the perfect webinar. Okay, so that was... That was... That'd be fun. As I was preparing, I said, this would be a lot of fun. I don't want to just teach the perfect webinar again. They can go buy that for seven bucks online and get the gist of it. Instead, I'm going to show it in action. So kind of like I did earlier today, I'm going to go into Russell Carrington and come back and forth and we'll have some fun. Does that sound good? Okay, it's a perfect webinar. So this is the framework. There's a couple core things, okay? You look at Garrett, shared his model, how he does his four core story. Very first section is what I call the origin story and the new opportunity, okay? Very similar to Garrett talked about. There's the origin story of how this thing came to be, right? So the first part is the origin story. Number two part is secret number one, which is the map. This is me explaining the map that gets them the actual result. Secret number two is the internal false beliefs, okay? What happens? First off, you show them the map. This is the thing I've got that's going to get you a result. They love the map and then they're like, I don't know if I can do it. So the second secret here is talking about how we break the false beliefs so they believe they can actually do it. The third part of the script then is helping them break their external false beliefs, okay? By the way, all these false beliefs are laying here inside this monkey, sorry, inside this elephant, okay? So this entire, this 15 minute or this three part of the whole presentation is all about talking directly to this monkey. These guys think I'm teaching them a whole bunch of really cool things, but it's just teaching through story so I can actually affect this guy right here, right? And then the last part is called the stack and the close. So those are the five of the webinar. We're basically all the stories we're doing, we talked about earlier, we're weaving them into this framework into a 90 minute presentation, okay? And that's kind of how it works, all right? Now the goal of the entire presentation is to do one thing and one thing only is I call it knocking down the big domino, okay? I think of them to believe one thing. It's not a goal or I'm not trying to do this webinar, I'm trying to get people to believe 10 or 20 or 30 different things. In fact, one of the biggest mistakes people make when they try to sell and this and this, okay? And they do this presentation and it gets so confusing because they're teaching all these different things, it doesn't work very well, okay? In fact, I remember talking to Perry Bellacher one time and he was showing me like 40 or 50 different video sellers there as they had done in a decade and he said, the most interesting thing he said, if any video seller tried to get them to believe more than one thing, the conversion has dropped in half. I tried to give them to believe three things, no one bought, okay? Every video, every presentation has to give them to believe one thing and one thing only. So what is the big domino? If I can then believe this one thing, it will knock down the big domino and everything else becomes irrelevant, okay? That's the goal when you're creating a presentation. So when I built the funnel hacks presentation initially for ClickFunnels, my goal was not like I'm going to believe, I'm going to believe this, this, this, this, this, it was like the number one thing I need to get them to believe. I get them to believe that funnels are the fastest way for them to actually grow their business, then they have to buy, right? That was it. So the entire presentation, all the stores, all of everything is all tied around that one thing, which is if I get them to believe that funnels are the fastest way to get them to the original that they want, then the big domino knocks down, boom, and they have to buy. They have to give you money or else they're literally, literally lying to themselves, okay? So that's the goal. So like I said, instead of just showing you, or instead of just teaching you this, I'm actually going to show you guys by actually doing it. And my goal is to kind of break the fourth wall, which they do in the office all the time, break the fourth wall. So the way you'll know, if the slide has, it looks like a normal slide like that, it means I'm in character, I'm Russell, and the slide has a red outline like this. That means that I am breaking the fourth wall and explaining something, okay? That way I also don't forget what I'm talking about. I'm in the middle of a presentation, I'm slipping through different slides, different stories. Okay, a couple of things at the end of this presentation, you guys are all going to have an opportunity. This is an opportunity that's very important. It's going to be scary for some of you guys. You're all going to have an opportunity, you're going to have an opportunity to invest in yourselves, in a decision, in something where you can actually move forward, right? Everything we talked about the last couple of days is amazing, but unless you decide to actually move forward, there's not anything else I can do for you, right? Those who pay pay attention. So we're going to give you an opportunity to invest. It's not going to be a low investment, the investment is going to be $25,000. And right now I'm buying me saying that, I guarantee you, this guy already started jumping like, 25 grand, I'm not going to pay that. It's too expensive and things, fears are already coming up, right? So before we get too deep in this, I want to remind you of a video I showed you earlier today, right? I don't ever ask people for money unless I think I can turn it into more money. That's it, right? You saw with Alex Hermosi, invested $25,000, made him a whole bunch more than that. Ladyboss, Brandon Cadenpole invested the same amount of money, made a whole bunch more than that. Okay. So I will actually make an investment. The good thing about it is that it's not a cost, it's an investment, right? But the results are huge. Okay. The results come back to this. What is the goal? What is the thing you want? Right? If you're our community, I'm guessing there's a whole bunch of things you want. But the way to define those things is typically a two-comic award, right? You want to win this two-comic award, you see it. That's the thing, right? You're conscious monkeys. They're like, I want to win that. I'm going to do that thing, right? You know what it is. You know what the goal is. You've set that goal over and over and over again, right? Here's the thing I'm going to go pursue. My goal right now is help to rewire the subconscious mind, the elephant, or the next 90 minutes so that it can be free so that you and your conscious mind can go and actually pursue the goals and the dreams you have. Okay. All right. So that's the game plan. Um, uh, so the very first thing I'm going to do now is I'm going to talk about this part of the perfect, uh, the perfect webinar. Okay. The very first part is how I introduce the new opportunity, how I learned the thing I'm going to be sharing with him, right? How I discovered it. Okay. So every time I'm selling anything, I'm always offering a result. You come back here with this map. There's your dream customer. There's a result right here. And the thing that you have to help them is the map, right? We talked about this today, number one. Okay. So during this first 15 minutes of a presentation, traditionally what I'm doing is I'm showing them the map. Here's the map where I discovered, I show how I discovered it. I tried during that period of time to rule out every other thing that they possibly could, could be trying to do to get the result. I want them to understand that the only way, the best way for them to get this results by following this map, how many of us have ever seen the Mandalorian? You know, the Mando's out there and they have this conversation. It looks like this is the way. Okay. My goal of the first 15 minutes, my presentation is to get them to say at the end, like this is the way the result I want is this, I want to build a big business. I want to lose a lot of weight. I want to do whatever the thing might be, right? They have a dream. They have a goal they want. Okay. And there's a whole bunch of different things they could potentially be doing for it, but by the time this first 15 minutes is done, my goal is for them to say, wow, if I'm going to get that result, this is the way. That's the entire goal of the first 15 minutes. Okay. So you guys go with that. Okay. So I'm going to jump into Russell presenter mode and we're going to have some fun. Okay. All right. I want to welcome you guys to the presentation. I'm pumped to be hanging out with you guys today. Today you guys are going to discover the one to many selling secrets that allowed me to sell over a billion dollars online. Yes, that's a billion with the B without a sales team. And the cool thing is you can learn how to do this. I can't really handle it this far away. Okay. You guys can read from where you're at. Okay. Now I'll tell you guys some story about this. Um, I remember, man, this is like 22, 23 years ago now, I went to my very first event and actually found some pictures of this is the very first event I ever went to. It was our memoirs, big seminar. Here's some pictures with me and my sweet shaved head. Uh, as I was at this event, of course, I wore tie because I thought business people had to wear ties and at this event at the time I had internet business making, I know, maybe $1,000 a month. If even that, I went to the event to learn in a marketing. I remember I got in the room and I was there with my glasses, my tie, my laptop. I sit down ready to take notes, the internet marketing event. And also all the people come in. There's like five or 600 people in the room and the first speaker gets on stage and I'm just so excited. Like I'm going to learn internet marketing. I'm going to drive traffic. I'm like, I'm so excited. Right. And the first guy got on stage. I still remember this day's name is Mike Littman. He got on stage, fairy cares, max speaker starts talking. He's going through his whole presentation. I'm taking notes. It was like most exciting thing in the world. Uh, I still remember vividly the entire presentation. It's kind of crazy. First time I ever seen this though, gets through the presentation and the end, he starts transitioning to this thing. And I'm like, what is he doing? He starts making an offer. He goes through the first thing that gives this and they're going to get this, you're going to get this and he does this whole thing. And he tells people, if you want to buy this product, run to the back of the room right now, it's $2,000 ready. He said, go. And I'm sitting there on my laptop. Like, what is happening? Like I'm like in this weird world. I've no idea what's happening. I'm watching people jump up and I'm watching them run back. Mike, that actually works. They're running to the back. This makes no logical sense. I'm watching him go back and I remember it was a $2,000 offer. I look in the back of the room and I'm counting the people around the table. Two, four, six, eight, 10, 12. I was like, this guy just made $60,000 in 90 minutes. And like for me, I got so far from any reality. Like I wasn't making $60,000 a year. I don't think my parents are making $60,000 a year. And this dude made it in an hour. I was like, okay, that was weird. Um, all right, next speaker gets up. I'm like, hey, now I can finally learn some internet marketing. Next guy gets up. He's doing his presentation. It's really good. Very charismatic. Going through the whole thing, telling these stories. I'm like, this is awesome. This is awesome. And then also he starts making an offer. I'm like, he's doing the same thing. What's going on? He starts making off, just talking through it. Also, he says the offer, the price is going to be $5,000. And also people start jumping up and start running to the back of the room. $120,000 in 90 minutes. I was like, people don't make that an entire year. It's not real. This is fake. I was like, there's no, like these people fake it. I cannot fathom. I couldn't figure it out. I'm doing the math. I'm just blown away. And then next guy gets up. He does presentation. Same thing happens. I just watch this for three days in a row. Day after day after day. And I remember sitting there thinking like, I thought I got into this business. I was going to be internet nerd. I'd be hiding in computer. I'd never talk to anybody. I was watching this happening. I was doing the math. And I was like, these guys are making a lot of money. I had to figure out what they're doing. I was like these guys are making a lot of money. I had to figure out what they're doing and then when the last speakers got up His name is John Chil
